posts.text:
“Roughly speaking, after the Iran war began and oil prices spiked, Russia’s additional daily oil revenues exceeded the amount of total annual investment in Ukraine’s entire defense-tech sector.” -Sergiy Kostman, Ukrainian defense consultant


Financial Times (@FT)

Moscow has so far earned an estimated $1.3bn-$1.9bn windfall from taxes on oil exports, making it the biggest winner from the conflict in the Middle East.

What does this mean for energy markets and the Russian economy? ft.trib.al/Yhy

posts.text:
The Trump administration either didn’t sufficiently forecast the risk of Russian and Chinese material gains from the Iran war, or didn’t prioritize them.


Javier Blas (@JavierBlas)

US has further eased Russian sanctions, allowing ALL countries (and not just India) to buy Russian oil already loaded by March 12. Vladimir Putin continues to cash in on President Trump's attack on Iran. The Kremlin can sell stranded oil barrels at much higher prices.







— https://nitter.net/JavierBlas/status/20

posts.text:
This is quite a power flex by Iran and China:


ian bremmer (@ianbremmer)

the straits of hormuz aren’t completely closed. 

iranian tankers are still passing through—bringing oil to china and oil revenue to the islamic republic. 

the united states could stop them. but that would antagonize china and further escalate the war. so…here we are.



— https://nitter.net/ianbremmer/status/2032106029060636989#m

posts.text: Trump has kind words for both Putin and Xi, saying Putin wants to be “constructive” in Ukraine peace talks and that it’s “an honor” for him to help China by easing oil traffic through the Gulf.
posts.quote: Trump has kind words for both Putin and Xi, saying Putin wants to be “constructive” in Ukraine peace talks and that it’s “an honor” for him to help China by easing oil traffic through the Gulf.

posts.text: Trump says that without US military action last year Iran “would have had a nuclear weapon and would have used it before this press conference.” The administration has not offered any intelligence or assessment to support this claim.

posts.text: Trump just used a new word for the war in Iran: “an excursion”.
